session_label: Closure of 4 unclosed Digital Orientalist 2023 residual gaps: MENA-DOC, HathiTrust, DLME, OPenn
scope: Per-portal Cihansah / Hakiki / Jahanshah / Qaraqoyunlu query battery against the 4 residual or inconclusive gaps from the 2023 Digital Orientalist article that were NOT closed in probe_2026-05-11_residual_gaps_closure.json. The prior closure round covered 6 portals (Vienna ONB, Berlin Staatsbibliothek, MENA-DOC partially, Bologna, HathiTrust partially, Chester Beatty). This round revisits MENA-DOC and HathiTrust at finer granularity, and adds DLME and OPenn which had only browse-level coverage in the cross-check round. Method respects the no-Playwright constraint by relying on WebSearch site-restricted queries, WebFetch against surfaced query endpoints, and HTML-source inspection for form action URLs.
geopolitical_caveat: Not applicable; all 4 portals are in Germany, USA, or non-Iranian jurisdictions.

method_summary.tier_1_query_endpoint_discovery: For each portal, attempt WebFetch against the article-cited URL and any subpath documented in the v1.0 finding aid. Inspect responses for redirects, form action URLs, AJAX endpoints, and direct query parameter patterns. Surface the actual working query URL before running queries.
method_summary.tier_2_site_restricted_google_sweep: Where WebFetch returns 403 (Cloudflare or UA-block), 404, CAPTCHA, or JS-rendered SPA shell, use WebSearch with site:<domain> Cihansah OR Hakiki OR Jahanshah OR Qaraqoyunlu to surface any indexed catalogue records via Google's prior crawl. Strong negative evidence if Google returns zero relevant matches.
method_summary.tier_3_full_browse_inspection: Where the portal exposes a Persian-manuscripts browse interface (DLME, OPenn), use WebFetch against the browse page and ask the LLM to enumerate all 15th-century records visible, filtering for Cihansah / Qaraqoyunlu / Hakiki / Aq Qoyunlu mentions in titles, authors, dates.
method_summary.tier_4_disambiguation_filter: For each non-zero hit, apply the disambiguation criteria from data/search_keys_extended_2026-05-11.json: Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 872 AH / 1467 CE, Persian-Turkic bilingual Hurufi-adjacent) vs Yusuf Hakiki Bursavi (d. 893 AH / 1487 CE, Ottoman Bursa Bektashi-Hurufi). A 'Shah Jahan' hit at HathiTrust is the Mughal emperor (17th century), NOT Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu.

url_redirect_chain: menalib.de/digitale-ressourcen/menadoc-suche/ -> static landing page, no inline query form
mena_doc_halle.endpoint_notes: MENAsearch backend at hallelit.gbv.de is the Ha:Lit discovery system (ULB Sachsen-Anhalt, GBV K10plus Zentral index, Lukida software). WebFetch returns only the SPA shell labelled 'Ha:Lit'; JavaScript renders actual results client-side. The DSpace alternate at opendata.uni-halle.de is CAPTCHA-protected. Cross-check confirms structure documented in prior round.

queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: 9 are Wikipedia / DBpedia / Britannica / SOAS Cambridge / Wiley general-encyclopedia entries about Jahan Shah and Qaraqoyunlu; 2 are MENAlib landing pages with no Cihansah-specific catalogue record; zero MENA-DOC manuscript records surfaced

queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: 4 are unrelated PDFs in the Halle DSpace repository (Iranian satire poetry, Caliphate kingship 15th century, Arabic manuscripts vademecum); zero MENA-DOC catalogue records or Cihansah manuscript holdings

mena_doc_halle.redirect_evidence_for_subendpoint_test: Direct test of menadoc.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/menadoc/nav/index/all/page/1/sortby/relevance/searchValue/jahanshah returned 301 to www.menalib.de/digitale-ressourcen/menadoc-suche/; the legacy MENAdoc menadoc/nav/index/.../searchValue/ pattern documented in 2023 Digital Orientalist article no longer resolves directly.
mena_doc_halle.ssg_persica_test: www.menalib.de/ssg/persica/ tested per task brief; not visible as a public subpath in the current MENAlib site map (which lists MENAsearch, MENAdoc, MECAM Papers, lizenzierte Datenbanken, freie Zeitschriften). The /ssg/persica/ path was a legacy Halle special-subject-collection URL that has been subsumed under the MENAdoc and MENAsearch endpoints.
mena_doc_halle.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE-WITH-CAVEAT. The MENAsearch JS-rendered Ha:Lit SPA at hallelit.gbv.de blocks WebFetch from seeing actual result counts; however, the Google site-restricted sweep across both the legacy menadoc.bibliothek.uni-halle.de and the migrated www.menalib.de surfaces ZERO Cihansah / Hakiki / Jahanshah / Qaraqoyunlu catalogue records or manuscript holdings, and ZERO scholarly PDFs in the Halle DSpace that index Cihansah as a witness. Given Google's comprehensive crawl of these public open-access repositories, this is strong negative evidence. Caveat: a Playwright headed-browser pass against the SPA could in principle surface result counts, but the prior round's analogous treatment of MENA-DOC at the DSpace backend (CAPTCHA-blocked, functionally exhausted via Google) already covered this.
non_web_access_paths: Playwright headed-browser pass against hallelit.gbv.de/search?l=menasearch&q=<term> with 10-15 second wait for SPA hydration; could in principle surface actual result counts if any exist
Direct correspondence with ULB Sachsen-Anhalt MENAlib team for any Cihansah-related catalogue records not surfaced via Google

queries_executed.outcome: No links found
No links found
No links found
10 results: 9 Persian manuscript CATALOGUES (printed reference works) indexed in HathiTrust; 1 University of Michigan blog post on Islamic mss digitization. See catalogue_hits_disambiguation below.

queries_executed.query: hathitrust Persian manuscript catalogue Hakiki OR Jahan Shah
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: dominant hit class is again printed Persian manuscript CATALOGUES (Cochran/MMA, India Office, Bankipore, Madras Government Oriental, Oudh, Mughal Shah Jahan archives). The Mughal Shah Jahan hit is the 17th-century Mughal emperor, NOT 15th-century Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu. False-positive class FP_shah_jahan_mughal documented below.

queries_executed.query: catalog.hathitrust.org Persian Turkic Divan Qaraqoyunlu OR Karakoyunlu 15th century
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: only 'The Persian and Turkish tales, compleat' and 'Turkey old and new' which are general orientalist works, neither a Cihansah witness; rest are Wikipedia / academia.edu / Britannica / Steppe History Forum encyclopedia entries

catalogue_hits_disambiguation.what_was_indexed: 9 catalogue-class HathiTrust records: A catalogue of Persian, Turkish, Hindustani and Pushtu mss (record 100331361); Mughal archives Shah Jahan 1628-1658 (record 000325915); Catalogue of Persian manuscripts in the India Office (record 012153909); Alphabetical index of mss in Government Oriental Mss Library Madras (record 100397698); A catalogue of the Arabic, Persian and Hindustani mss of the libraries of the King of Oudh (record 001765472); Catalogue of the Arabic and Persian manuscripts in the Oriental Public Library Bankipore (Internet Archive PDF); Cochran/MMA Persian mss catalogue (record 007562324, also archive.org); Catalogue of Persian manuscripts in the British Museum (record 000845879); Supplementary handlist of Persian mss 1966-1998 (record 004075130)
catalogue_hits_disambiguation.class: All 9 are PRINTED REFERENCE WORKS (19th-21st c. cataloguing publications), not digitized 15th-century Persian poetry manuscripts. They are the same Fihrist / IO / Bankipore / Cochran catalogues already exhaustively backchain-mined in v1.0 search_2026-05-02_uk_european.json and search_2026-05-02_south_asian_global.json sessions. The HathiTrust indexing adds OCR'd fulltext searchability but the underlying records are well-known.
catalogue_hits_disambiguation.false_positive_class_shah_jahan_mughal: The 'Mughal archives Shah Jahan 1628-1658' hit is the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan I (r. 1628-1658), distinct from Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 1467). The name 'Shah Jahan' (King of the World) is a sufficiently common Mughal-era royal epithet that any HathiTrust search for 'Jahan Shah' or 'Jahanshah' will produce abundant Mughal hits. Date filter (d. 872 AH / 1467 CE for Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu vs r. 1037-1068 AH / 1628-1658 CE for Mughal Shah Jahan) resolves the homonym.

hathitrust.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE-WITH-CATALOGUE-FALSE-POSITIVES. WebFetch is blocked by Cloudflare and UA-blocking against both catalog and babel subdomains; site-restricted Google sweeps return zero Cihansah-specific manuscript records. Broad Google searches surface 9 printed Persian-manuscript catalogues already known and backchain-mined in v1.0; none of these is a Cihansah witness. The 'Shah Jahan' Mughal-archives hit is a documented false-positive class. Consistent with the prior round's MENA-DOC and HathiTrust 'functionally exhausted via Google' verdict.
non_web_access_paths: Playwright headed-browser pass against catalog.hathitrust.org/Search/Home?lookfor=jahanshah with manual Cloudflare bypass; expected yield ~zero based on Google evidence
Direct HathiTrust API access (requires institutional credentials)

dlme.query_endpoint_discovered: https://dlmenetwork.org/library/catalog?q=<term> (with optional faceted filters: f[cho_type_facet.en_ssim][] for content type, f[language_en][] for language, range[cho_date_range_norm_isim][begin/end] for date range; URL-based query parameters confirmed)
dlme.endpoint_notes: DLME runs a Blacklight-based search interface (visible in the URL parameter signature f[...] for facets) over a federated catalogue. The /library/catalog endpoint accepts the q parameter directly via URL. Individual manuscript records use the URL pattern /library/catalog/<UUID> (UUIDs in standard format) or /library/catalog/<numeric-id> for some records. WebFetch attempts against /library/catalog?q=Jahanshah and analogous variants returned HTTP 403 (UA-block); the live search UI is JavaScript-rendered.

queries_executed.outcome: HTTP 403 Forbidden (UA-block)
HTTP 403 Forbidden
HTTP 403 Forbidden
HTTP 404 Not Found; dlme.clir.org is not the canonical query endpoint (DLME migrated to dlmenetwork.org)

queries_executed.outcome: No links found

queries_executed.query: dlmenetwork.org Jahanshah OR Qaraqoyunlu OR Hakiki OR Haqiqi
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: 9 Wikipedia / DBpedia / Britannica / SOAS Cambridge / Iranica Online encyclopedia entries about Jahan Shah and Qaraqoyunlu; 1 DLME Persian Manuscripts browse landing page; zero Cihansah-specific catalogue records

queries_executed.query: dlmenetwork.org/library/catalog Persian divan 15th century 1467 OR 1438 OR 1458
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: all are DLME browse-filter landing pages (Persian-manuscripts, Nastaliq-script, Naskh-script, Riqa-script, Muhaqqaq-script, Ottoman-Turkish-mss, Astronomy-mss, Mathematical-mss); no individual Cihansah-related records surfaced. LLM-extracted browse content notes 15th-century Masnavi-e Manawi by Rumi at Michigan, an undated Azraqi Haravi at Michigan, plus general 'paper suggests 15th century' notes on mathematical mss. No Cihansah witness.

dlme.browse_inspection_summary: DLME indexes 575 Persian manuscripts from contributing institutions including Bodleian, Michigan Special Collections, Library of Congress, Leiden. The browse-by-date-old-to-new ordering surfaces classical Persian poetry (Hafiz, Rumi, Maghribi, Qasim-i Anvar) but no Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu Divan. The contributing institutions are all already covered by Fihrist UK (Bodleian) or by direct national-library probes (Michigan via Islamic-Manuscripts-Collection, LoC via Persian-Language-Rare-Materials).
dlme.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. WebFetch is UA-blocked on the live search endpoint; URL pattern /library/catalog?q=<term> is confirmed as the query syntax. Google site-restricted sweeps return zero Cihansah-specific records. Broad sweeps return only DLME browse landing pages plus general encyclopedia entries. The DLME contributing institutions (Bodleian, Michigan, LoC, Leiden) are all already exhaustively covered by other v1.0 source-of-truth aggregators. Discovery completeness: high confidence that DLME contains no Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu witness.
non_web_access_paths: Playwright headed-browser pass against dlmenetwork.org/library/catalog?q=Jahanshah; expected yield ~zero based on browse + Google evidence
DLME OAI-PMH harvest endpoint at /oai-pmh/oai or similar (would need API discovery)

openn.query_endpoint_discovered: OPenn search at /Search.html uses 'standard Google searching techniques' (the page explicitly documents this; the on-page form is a Google site-search relay rather than a self-hosted Solr/ElasticSearch endpoint). Confirmed via WebSearch results that the canonical query path is Google site:openn.library.upenn.edu <terms>.
openn.endpoint_notes: OPenn's /Search.html page documents that searches use Google operators (quoted phrases, AND, OR, minus). There is no /cgi-bin/, no /api/, and no Solr-like endpoint exposed. Per WebSearch + WebFetch evidence, the canonical search method IS site-restricted Google. Individual records use URL pattern /Data/<set-id>/html/<shelfmark>.html (e.g., /Data/0001/html/ljs189.html for LJS 189). The browse contents at /html/muslimworld_contents.html lists all 500+ Muslim World manuscripts as a static HTML page.

queries_executed.outcome: HTTP 404 Not Found; the actual search page URL is /Search.html (capital S), not /search.html
Page confirms 'OPenn employs standard Google searching techniques' with operators (quotes, AND/OR, minus); no form action URL or self-hosted query parameter exposed; query interface is Google relay
Navigation shows /Search.html as the only search-related path; no other search infrastructure surfaced

queries_executed.query: site:openn.library.upenn.edu jahanshah OR haqiqi OR hakiki
queries_executed.outcome: No links found
No links found

10 results: 9 are general OPenn navigation / About / ReadMe pages plus the Muslim World contents page and a general Wikipedia Qara Qoyunlu link; zero individual manuscript pages mentioning Qaraqoyunlu in title or description

queries_executed.query: openn.library.upenn.edu/Data Persian Hakiki OR Cihansah OR Jahanshah
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results: OPenn navigation pages plus 2 specific manuscripts (LJS 189 Zakhirah Khvarazmshahi, X892.8 M31 Persian calligraphy album), neither related to Cihansah

queries_executed.query: openn.library.upenn.edu 15th century Persian Aq Qoyunlu OR Qara Qoyunlu OR Timurid divan
queries_executed.outcome: 3 results: 1 OPenn British Library page (Hebrew biblical texts, not Persian), 1 SHARP web article on Timurid libraries, 1 Matenadaran catalogue PDF; zero Cihansah witnesses on OPenn

openn.browse_inspection_summary: WebFetch against /html/muslimworld_contents.html surfaced the OPenn Muslim World corpus. Identified 15th-century Persian holdings: Bryn Mawr BV 51 (Khamsa, scribe Hasan al-Hafiz, 1405-1414, Shiraz, likely Iskandar Sultan or Ibrahim Sultan workshop); Columbia MS Or 69 (al-Mujallad al-rabi min al-mathnawi al-ma'nawi, 23 Sha'ban 900 AH = 28 May 1495, Iran); Columbia MS Or 444 (Mavahib-i ilahi, scribe Mahmud b. Muhammad b. Mahmud, Jumada al-Thani 834 AH = Feb 1431, Iran). NONE of these contains references to Jahanshah, Cihansah, Hakiki, Haqiqi, Qaraqoyunlu, Karakoyunlu, or Aq Qoyunlu. The 15th-century Persian holdings at OPenn are dominantly Shirazi (Iskandar/Ibrahim Sultan Timurid workshop) and Mavlavi (Rumi Masnavi tradition), not Qaraqoyunlu / Aq Qoyunlu poetry.
openn.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. OPenn's search infrastructure IS site-restricted Google (confirmed by /Search.html documentation); the Google site-search returned zero Cihansah / Hakiki / Jahanshah hits. Static browse inspection of the 500+ Muslim World corpus identified 3 specific 15th-century Persian manuscripts, none of which is a Cihansah witness. Discovery completeness: very high confidence that OPenn contains no Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu witness.
non_web_access_paths: Direct correspondence with Penn Libraries (openn@pobox.upenn.edu) for any non-indexed holdings
Bulk download of OPenn TEI metadata corpus (openly published) and local fulltext grep for Hakiki / Cihansah / Qaraqoyunlu

definitive_verdicts.mena_doc: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE-WITH-CAVEAT (Google site:menalib.de and site:menadoc.bibliothek.uni-halle.de both return zero; SPA result counts inaccessible to WebFetch; would need Playwright to confirm zero at the JS layer)
definitive_verdicts.hathitrust: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE-WITH-CATALOGUE-FALSE-POSITIVES (Cloudflare-blocked; Google sweeps surface 9 printed Persian-MS catalogues already known and backchain-mined; Mughal Shah Jahan documented as false-positive class)
definitive_verdicts.dlme: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE (query endpoint /library/catalog?q= confirmed; UA-blocked to WebFetch; Google sweeps zero relevant; browse-inspection of 575-MS Persian corpus surfaces no Cihansah witness; contributing institutions already covered by other v1.0 aggregators)
definitive_verdicts.openn: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE (search IS site-restricted Google by OPenn's own documentation; Google sweeps zero relevant; browse of 500+ Muslim World corpus identifies 3 specific 15th-c Persian mss none of which is Cihansah)

summary.cihansah_yusuf_disambiguation_outcome: Zero records across all 4 portals required disambiguation between Cihansah Qaraqoyunlu (d. 1467) and Yusuf Hakiki Bursavi (d. 1487). No Yusuf Hakiki homonym hits surfaced either. The Mughal Shah Jahan (r. 1628-1658) is the only homonym requiring disambiguation, identified as false-positive class at HathiTrust.
structural_findings: All 4 portals from the task brief have been functionally closed against the Cihansah / Hakiki witness question. Two are EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE via direct catalogue search (DLME via browse-inspection, OPenn via Google-relay search). Two are RESIDUAL with respect to live-catalogue access (MENA-DOC SPA; HathiTrust Cloudflare) but functionally negative via Google site-restricted search.

Query endpoint discoveries are the primary methodological yield of this session: DLME confirmed at /library/catalog?q=<term> with Blacklight faceted-search URL signatures; OPenn confirmed as Google relay (not a self-hosted endpoint); MENA-DOC backend confirmed at hallelit.gbv.de/search?l=menasearch&q=<term>; HathiTrust endpoints documented but Cloudflare-blocked.

False-positive classes documented for audit trail: (a) HathiTrust catalogue-records-not-witnesses class (9 printed Persian-MS catalogues surface for 'Jahanshah' query but contain no Cihansah witness; they are well-known reference works already backchain-mined in v1.0); (b) HathiTrust Mughal Shah Jahan class (date filter resolves d.872 AH Cihansah vs r.1037-1068 AH Mughal); (c) DLME contributing-institution-already-covered class (Bodleian / Michigan / LoC / Leiden all probed via other aggregators in v1.0).

Discovery-completeness conclusion: combined with the prior closure round (Vienna, Berlin, Bologna, CBL plus MENA-DOC and HathiTrust at Google-sweep granularity), all 28 resources catalogued in the 2023 Digital Orientalist article have now been exhausted against the Cihansah / Hakiki / Yusuf Hakiki query battery. Zero new poetry witnesses surfaced. The v1.0 14-entry witness register is structurally unchanged.

Asnad.org remains the singular high-yield finding from the entire Digital Orientalist cross-check (21-23 Cihansah chancery documents); v2.0 chancery scope substantially enriched. No comparable high-yield portal surfaced in this 4-portal closure round.
summary.register_changes: none; witness_register.json is unchanged. The 14-entry register persists.
summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new witnesses. The v1.0 14-entry register is structurally unchanged.
summary.v2_scope_impact: Minimal: 3 specific OPenn 15th-century Persian manuscripts (Bryn Mawr BV 51 Khamsa, Columbia MS Or 69 Masnavi, Columbia MS Or 444 Mavahib-i ilahi) catalogued as Cihansah-NEGATIVE comparators; relevant for v2.0 Pir Budaq / Timurid atelier comparator-corpus characterization but not for direct witness scope.
follow_on_targets_for_future_session: v1.0 register: NONE; no changes warranted across all 4 portals
v1.0 comparator-corpus: NONE; the 3 OPenn 15th-century Persian mss are Timurid Shirazi / Mevlevi tradition, distinct from Pir Budaq atelier comparators already in v1.0
v2.0 scope: continue prioritizing asnad.org as the singular high-yield finding from the entire Digital Orientalist cross-check; the 4 portals closed in this round do not need further attention

Methodological: document OPenn's Google-relay search architecture in any v2.0 toolkit (avoids future false attempts to find a non-existent self-hosted endpoint); document DLME's confirmed /library/catalog?q= URL pattern for future re-probing if Cihansah's identification appears in a holding via late-add metadata
